title: Traditional British Bean Pie
description: A classic bean pie originating from the UK, made with broad beans and a hint of British flair.
instructions: Roll out the shortcrust pastry to a thickness of about 3mm.
Place the pastry into a 20cm pie dish, trimming any excess.
In a pan, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened.
Add the broad beans to the pan and cook for 2-3 minutes.
Season with flaky salt and black pepper to taste.
Pour the bean mixture into the pastry-lined pie dish.
Roll out the remaining pastry to a thickness of about 3mm.
Place the rolled-out pastry on top of the bean mixture, pressing the edges to seal.
Cut a few slits in the top pastry to allow steam to escape.
Brush the top pastry with a little water.
Bake the pie in the preheated oven for 35-40 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown.
